fields.description:
# Code-grounding evidence oracle (v1: T0+T1 floor) — polyglot, deterministic, fail-open, pure-evidence

## Context

Both the plan-review gate (`5fd2`) and the three-pass code reviewers (`9da1`) need to GROUND findings in the
actual code — deterministically, polyglot, and fail-open. This epic builds that shared capability ONCE as a
reusable internal library so the two review efforts don't implement it twice.

It is a **pure EVIDENCE oracle**: it returns evidence (outcome + structured reason + provenance/coverage) and
NEVER decides block/advisory — that policy lives entirely in the consuming code (out of scope here). Every
wrapper FAILS OPEN: unsupported language / missing tool / crash / timeout → an `abstain` with a recorded reason
(never a false accusation), so coverage is honest and a client who doesn't adopt it pays nothing.

Buy-vs-build was checked: **no reusable WHOLE exists** (the AI-review field is closed-SaaS, verdict-emitting,
fail-CLOSED, FP-minimizing — the opposite posture). The gap is benign: the underlying pieces are mature and
reusable, so this epic is a **thin integration over reused backends** plus the novel contract. We reuse
aggressively at the backend layer and build only the integration + the evidence/abstain contract.

## The contract (locked)

- **Three-valued, confirm-only.** Every probe returns `resolved` OR `abstained(reason)` — and on the resolution
  lane the resolved verdict is **`refuted`** (we found it → the agent's absence claim is a false positive),
  never an asserted absence. You can't prove an absence; you can only DISPROVE an asserted one. So the oracle
  reduces false positives; it never manufactures confirmations.
- **Structured abstention reason + coverage** (SMT `unknown`-with-reason model): `abstain(reason = unsupported_lang
  | no_tool | parse_error | timeout | ambiguous | private_or_internal_suspected | network_error | rate_limited
  | version_skew | invalid_detector | other)`. The enum is CLOSED on the load-bearing reasons (no open `…`):
  `version_skew` (the #1 real failure) and `invalid_detector` (a project detector failing validation) are
  first-class; `other` is the explicit catch-all. Skips are recorded so callers can report coverage. Fail-open
  is the IRONCLAD invariant.
- **One normalized evidence model; SARIF at the edges.** Internally one finding/evidence shape across all
  backends; SARIF 2.1.0 is used for interchange/ingest (OpenGrep emits it) but our three-valued model is a
  superset (SARIF has no native `refuted`/`abstain`/coverage), mapped to/from SARIF at the boundary.
- **Reference-in contract.** The oracle does NOT extract references from prose (non-deterministic → consumer's
  LLM job). It checks EXPLICIT, structured references the review agent emits; deterministic extraction FROM CODE
  (parse a diff/file) is an optional in-scope helper. Separate candidate-generation (recall, model-driven) from
  verification (precision, deterministic) — the oracle is strictly the verification side.

## Three jobs over a full repo checkout

1. **Refute an asserted absence** (resolution lane). Given a structured reference (import/symbol/dependency a
   reviewer claims is hallucinated/invalid), try to DISPROVE it against the repo + its declared deps.
   - **v1 = a deterministic T1 floor** = a **universal-ctags** repo-wide tags index (164 langs, no server, no
     build) for symbol/definition existence + plain file-path existence + manifest/registry dependency existence
     (T0). (grep-ast is NOT a repo-wide indexer — it is a per-file AST-context grep; the repo-map/tags machinery
     lives in *aider*, not the package — so grep-ast is at most an optional context-display helper, NOT the
     index. Spike: docs/experiments/code-grounding-spike/.) LSP/SCIP semantic resolution is **T2 — deferred** to
     `850f` (servers/builds, non-deterministic).
   - **The T1 `refuted` verdict is SCOPED to name-existence** (bare symbol / import name / dependency name — the
     high-base-rate hallucination class). Member/attribute/signature resolution (`foo.bar` on a type) and
     common-name-collision cases are coarse at T1 and **abstain to T2** rather than risk a false-refute. (Spike
     on a 4-language fixture: 100% yield, 0 false-refute, clean fail-open — but only for distinct names; yield is
     re-measured on a real corpus in the eval, not asserted.)
   - There is NO single off-the-shelf polyglot fail-open resolver (GitHub stack-graphs is ARCHIVED; multilspy
     needs servers; SCIP needs builds) — so job 1 is a TIERED resolver we orchestrate; universal-ctags is the
     always-available floor that makes day-one fail-open achievable.
2. **Self-declared applicability detection** (Engine B). Deterministic "does review dimension X apply to this
   repo?" triggers (is-web-project / has-IaC / touches-auth), STRUCTURAL not naive-grep, more reliable. A
   detector's own applicability declaration IS the deterministic trigger (`5fd2`/`9da1` overlay routing).
3. **Smell / architectural-decay scans** (Engine B). LOW-threshold attention routers that FLAG AREAS for an LLM
   to inspect closely — recall-oriented, not blocking. (scc/lizard size/complexity feeds this + plan-review P4.)

Jobs 2 and 3 are the SAME machine (run declarative patterns over the repo, emit matches); the only difference
is what the consumer does with a match (consumer policy, out of scope).

## Reuse map (confirmed)

- **Engine B backend = OpenGrep** (the permissive LGPL-2.1 fork of Semgrep — engine AND rules, 30+ languages,
  JSON+SARIF). Its custom + project-local rule loading IS the registry substrate — reuse it; do NOT re-invent a
  match DSL. This also dissolves the Semgrep-rule licensing trap (Semgrep CE's Rules License is non-OSI). ast-grep
  is an optional lighter secondary matcher.
- **Detector format = thin envelope + verbatim native payload** (Trivy model), NOT a cross-compiler (Sigma's
  compiler half is overkill for our inverse case). A detector = `{id, metadata, languages/applicability,
  severity-hint, backend discriminator, evidence-mapping}` + a native matcher payload (OpenGrep rule | ast-grep
  block | manifest predicate | metric threshold) passed through unchanged.
- **Job 1 floor = universal-ctags** repo-wide tags index (deterministic, no server; 164 langs). tree-sitter (via
  tree-sitter-language-pack, ~306 grammars) backs structural/detector parsing. grep-ast is NOT an indexer
  (per-file context only) → optional display helper, not the floor. **Reject** MegaLinter/Super-Linter/pre-commit
  as Engine B (CI runners, fail-closed, not embeddable). **Avoid** stack-graphs (archived).
- **Metrics = scc / lizard** as a metric matcher type.
- **Interchange = SARIF 2.1.0**; our evidence model is the superset.

## Registry conventions (from prior-art convergence)

- Built-in (official, shipped) detectors + **project-local detectors auto-discovered from `.rebar/detectors/`**,
  unioned at load; **namespaced** built-in vs project IDs; **last-wins / explicit override**; project config
  expresses deltas only (enable/disable/severity per ID, per-path globs); absent dir = no error (fail-open).
- **Applicability = self-declared + file-presence routing** (Trivy/MegaLinter) — absent language/files → skipped
  silently+cheaply (the polyglot + optional property, for free).
- **Fail-open = each backend in a recover/timeout boundary** (golangci-lint `analyzeSafe`): crash/absent/
  version-skew → skip + RECORD it (the visible skip IS the coverage record; never a silent no-op).
- **The recover boundary is rebar's, NOT the engine's.** Spike finding: OpenGrep/semgrep ABORTS the whole run on
  a schema-invalid rule (exit 7) rather than skipping it — so rebar's loader must **pre-validate + quarantine**
  each detector (recorded `abstain(invalid_detector)`) BEFORE invoking the engine. (Target-file parse errors are
  different — the engine skips-and-continues those.)
- **Fail-open spans out-of-process AND in-process backends.** Out-of-process tools (ctags, ast-grep, OpenGrep,
  scc/lizard, registry HTTP) are reaped via the subprocess pattern (`acli_subprocess`: process-group,
  SIGTERM→SIGKILL, timeout). In-process bindings (tree-sitter) CANNOT be bounded that way — a thread/signal
  timeout can't interrupt a hung C-extension call and a segfault would kill the host — so **prefer the CLI form
  of every matcher** (all have one) and run any unavoidable in-process binding inside a **worker subprocess**.
- **Version skew is the #1 real failure** — pin/record each backend version; mismatch → `abstain(version_skew)`.
- v1 distribution = built-in + `.rebar/detectors/` only; a shareable rule-pack registry/lockfile is DEFERRED.
- Detector discovery/loading is owned HERE; the project-config file convention is COORDINATED with `55de`
  (config tool/UX), not blocked on it. This layer is NOT a duplicate of `3156` (project-supplied LLM criteria).

## Polyglot & optional (must-holds)

- Polyglot, not Python-indexed: tree-sitter tags across grammars (not Python `ast`); purl ecosystems for deps
  (npm/PyPI/crates/Go/Maven/RubyGems/NuGet); multi-framework web detection (React/Vue/Angular/Svelte + Rails/
  Django templates); detectors carry a `languages`/`applies_to` field so a rule never fires on the wrong stack.
- Optional & non-impacting: gated behind an optional extra; core stays import-clean (the existing optionality CI
  gate); a non-adopting client pays nothing — engines aren't imported, any query fails open to abstain/no-op.

## Language support — ownership (R-7: we own the common case; the user owns the long tail; fail-open under both)

- **We own the common case (user does nothing).** The bundled engines already cover the overwhelming majority:
  universal-ctags (164 langs) + tree-sitter-language-pack (~306 grammars) — e.g. a COBOL project resolves out of
  the box (ctags ships `Cobol`; the grammar pack ships `cobol`). OpenGrep covers ~30 langs natively + a `generic`
  text mode.
- **The user owns the genuine long tail — via the engines' NATIVE extension mechanisms, surfaced through a
  documented rebar slot we read.** We never go find or build a parser. For a language no bundled engine parses:
  (a) cheapest — a **ctags optlib** (regex-based `--langdef`/`--regex-<LANG>` in a `*.ctags` file or an
  `--optlib-dir`; no recompile) gives the symbol-existence floor; (b) richer — a **tree-sitter grammar**
  registered the way ast-grep's `customLanguages` does, for structural detectors. The project declares these in a
  rebar config slot (under the `.rebar/` convention, coordinated with `55de`) pointing at the optlib/grammar
  path; the loader passes them through to the engines.
- **Fail-open is the floor under all of it.** A language neither we nor the user configured → `abstain
  (unsupported_lang)` with the coverage gap recorded — never a wrong answer. An unconfigured exotic-language
  project simply abstains on those files; everything else still resolves.

## Plan-review dispositions (round 1 — self-review via the `5fd2` three-pass gate; log `straw-larva-vigor`)

The gate produced 6 advisory findings (0 blocks). Disposition:
- #1/#2 (unvalidated reuse/yield bets) — RESOLVED by an experimental spike (task `risk-leg-churn`;
  docs/experiments/code-grounding-spike/): ctags floor = 100% yield / 0 false-refute polyglot; grep-ast corrected
  to universal-ctags (R-A); T1 refute scoped to name-existence (R-B).
- #3 in-process fail-open (R-3), #5 `version_skew` in the reason enum (R-5), #6 configurable metric thresholds
  (R-6), plus the loader-owns-recover-boundary correction (R-C), and the R-7 language-ownership model — all
  folded into this design + the affected children (S1/S2/S4/S6).
- #4 horizontal big-bang (no thin vertical slice before all engines): VALID but **ACCEPTED WITH OVERRIDE** for
  v1 — the engines share one small contract (S1) and the integration risk is judged acceptable here; revisit if
  S5 integration surfaces contract churn.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A reusable internal API exposes the three jobs (refute-absence, applies?, smell-scan) over a full repo,
      polyglot and fail-open, returning the normalized three-valued evidence model (outcome + reason + coverage).
- [ ] Every wrapper fails open: unsupported lang / missing tool / crash / timeout → `abstain` with a recorded
      reason; coverage is reconstructable; the resolution lane only ever `refuted`/`abstain`, never asserts absence.
- [ ] Detector registry supports built-in + project-local (`.rebar/detectors/`) detectors via the thin-envelope
      format over OpenGrep (+ ast-grep/manifest/metric) backends; self-declared applicability; namespaced IDs.
- [ ] Consumed by `5fd2`'s DET floor (P2/P3/P4) and the `9da1` reviewers — one implementation, not two.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shared evidence contract (three-valued + structured reason + coverage) + fail-open recover/timeout harness
      + optional-extra scaffolding (import-clean core), implemented and the dependency root for the rest.
- [ ] Engine A refutation resolver (**universal-ctags** T1 floor: file-path + repo-wide symbol-NAME existence,
      confirm-only; member/signature/collision abstain to T2) + the explicit reference-in schema; refutation yield
      + false-refute measured on a real corpus (not asserted); LSP/SCIP + grep-ast-as-index explicitly out.
- [ ] T0 dependency/package-existence (manifest + purl-ecosystem registries) with the abstain gauntlet
      (normalize + workspace/monorepo + stdlib + import-vs-distribution + transient-error → abstain, never absent).
- [ ] Engine B detector registry + OpenGrep evaluator (+ ast-grep secondary, + scc/lizard metric matcher),
      thin-envelope format, project-local discovery, self-declared applicability, SARIF-normalized evidence.
- [ ] Stable public oracle API exposing the three query surfaces + the consumer integration/reference contract,
      consumed by `5fd2` and `9da1`; docs for the API, fail-open behavior, the detector format, and `.rebar/detectors/`.
- [ ] Fail-open conformance suite + polyglot fixture corpus: every backend tested for unsupported-lang / no-tool /
      crash / timeout / version-skew / invalid-detector → `abstain` with coverage recorded; in-process backends
      fail-open under a worker boundary; a non-adopting client is unaffected. PLUS a refutation-yield + false-refute
      eval on a real multi-language corpus (seeded by docs/experiments/code-grounding-spike/) so yield is measured.
- [ ] Language extensibility verified: a project-supplied ctags optlib (and/or tree-sitter custom grammar) for an
      otherwise-unsupported language is read from the `.rebar/` slot and used; an unconfigured language fails open.
